text: When building a website, security should always be a priority. Using HTTPS encrypts data sent between the user and the server, protecting sensitive information from attackers. User authentication, such as passwords or two-factor login, ensures only authorized people can access accounts. However, even with these measures, developers must validate all user input, as unchecked fields can allow harmful code or database attacks. Common vulnerabilities include cross-site scripting (XSS) and SQL injection, where malicious input steals data or corrupts databases. By following secure coding practices—like sanitizing inputs and keeping software updated—developers can greatly reduce risks and build trust with their users.
